learning_outcomes.description: Explain the macroeconomics variables and the notion of equilibrium in the goods market and the determination of output.
Explain the labour market equilibrium and the equilibrium in all three markets - goods, financial and labour - together.
Explain the interplay between capital accumulation and growth, and explain the impact of technological progress on the growth rate of economies.
Explain the real interest rate, nominal interest rate, and expected present discount value.
Explain the implications of openness in goods markets and financial markets, and also know equilibrium in the goods market in an open economy.
Utilise the IS-LM model to elucidate the impacts on the open economy.

assessments.due_date:
Quiz 1: 3/09/2026 12:00 pm

Quiz 2: 17/09/2026 12:00 pm

Quiz 3: 8/10/2026 12:00 pm

Quiz 4: 22/10/2026 12:00 pm

30 minute quiz conducted during the regular lecture time.
assessments.task_description:
30 minute quiz conducted during the regular lecture time.

There are 4 lecture quizzes worth 10% each. Students choose any 3 of these tests for a total of 30% assessment.

The quizzes will cover the previous week's lecture topic. E.g. The quiz in Week 6 is based on the lecture in Week 5.

No deferred quizzes will be offered.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit in hard copy during the lecture.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: Students are required to write an analysis of the Reserve Bank of Australia Statement on Monetary Policy, February 2026. Students are given a set of specific questions requiring an explanation of various graphs and statements in the RBA’s document in terms of the models, concepts and economic mechanisms that have been covered in the course. The assignment covers all topics in the course. Further details will be provided.
assessments.submission_guidelines: To be submitted by the due date and time via turnitin, BB.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The exam will be 2 hours duration, closed book and will cover all content in the course.

It will consist of 7 short answer questions of which students must answer 5, for 8 marks each.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
